provenance.title: Stochastic Policy Gradient Methods in the Uncertain Volatility Model
affiliations: Université Paris Cité and Sorbonne Université (LPSM, UMR CNRS 8001)
ENSAE-CREST and Institut Polytechnique de Paris
provenance.abstract_truncated: Backward actor-critic stochastic policy gradient scheme for multidimensional Uncertain Volatility Model. Combines discrete dynamic programming with PPO and shallow neural networks. Policy uses C-vine representation enforcing positive semidefiniteness. Numerical experiments show accurate robust option prices.
key_claims: Backward actor-critic SPG scheme for multi-dimensional UVM combining DPP with PPO yields accurate prices with moderate runtimes
C-vine parameterization of correlation matrices enforces positive semidefiniteness by construction without projections or Cholesky checks
Squashed Gaussian (continuous) and factorized Bernoulli (bang-bang) policy classes tailored to UVM structural constraints
Method compares favorably with GTU and NNU benchmarks from Goudenège, Molent, Zanette (2024)
Scales to d=80 assets in fixed-correlation setting; tested up to d=5 with uncertain correlations (15-dim control)

architecture.description: Backward actor-critic framework with two shallow feedforward neural networks per time step: Actor (policy net) and Critic (value net). N time steps processed sequentially backward.

components.name: Actor (Policy Network)
components.role: Outputs latent Gaussian mean (continuous policy) or Bernoulli probabilities (bang-bang policy)

components.name: Critic (Value Network)
components.role: Approximates value function V_n(x) at each backward time step

components.name: TUVM Transform (Continuous Policy)
operations: tanh squashing of full latent vector
Volatility components: affine rescaling from (-1,1) to (sigma_min^i, sigma_max^i)
Correlation components: C-vine parameterization mapping partial correlations to valid PSD correlation matrix
components.role: Enforces admissible action constraints; output (sigma, rho) used directly in log-Euler scheme

components.name: Bang-Bang Policy Layer
operations: Sigmoid activation to produce Bernoulli parameters q_i in (0,1)
Map a_i in {0,1} to sigma_i = sigma_min + a_i*(sigma_max - sigma_min)
components.role: Discrete volatility selection; linear in d (not exponential)

components.name: Log-Euler Scheme (F)
components.input_shape: [B, d] x [B, d, d] x [B, d]
components.role: Simulates one discrete time step of multi-asset GBM under control a

training_variants.name: Continuous (Squashed Gaussian)
training_variants.description: Full correlation and volatility uncertainty; uses TUVM + C-vine; correlation penalty for constraint enforcement

training_variants.name: Bang-Bang (Bernoulli)
training_variants.description: Volatility uncertainty only at extremes; correlation handled separately (exact in d<=2); lighter and faster

ambiguities.description: Covariance matrix Lambda of latent Gaussian is scalar lambda*I; exact annealing schedule values not uniformly specified
ambiguities.primary_assumption: lambda annealed from 1.0 to 0.01 via sigmoid schedule

equations.name: Asset dynamics (continuous-time UVM)
equations.role: Model definition; alpha_s is the volatility matrix control

equations.name: Admissible control set A
equations.role: Defines feasible control space for volatilities and correlations

equations.name: Robust pricing / value function
equations.role: The option's worst-case (robust) price under uncertain volatility

equations.name: Black-Scholes-Barenblatt PDE (HJB)
equations.latex: \partial_t V + r(x^\top \nabla_x V - V) + \frac{1}{2}\sup_{a\in A} \mathrm{Tr}[\nabla^2_x V \,\mathrm{diag}(x)\, aa^\top\, \mathrm{diag}(x)] = 0
equations.role: PDE characterization (used for motivation; NOT used in numerical scheme)

equations.name: Discrete-time DPP (Bellman recursion)
equations.role: Core backward recursion; replaced by PPO actor-critic approximation

equations.name: Statewise performance criterion
equations.role: Actor objective at each time step n

equations.name: Score function / REINFORCE gradient
equations.role: Policy gradient estimator; baseline-subtracted with critic V_phi

equations.name: Advantage
equations.role: Reduces variance in policy gradient; used in PPO clipped objective

equations.name: PPO clipped surrogate objective
equations.role: Main actor loss; r_theta = p_theta / p_theta_old is likelihood ratio

equations.name: Critic loss
equations.role: MSE regression loss for value network

equations.name: Continuous policy volatility squashing
equations.role: Maps unconstrained latent z to bounded volatility

equations.name: C-vine correlation map (d=3 example)
equations.role: Converts partial correlations to standard correlations, ensuring PSD

equations.name: Correlation penalty (Huber)
equations.role: Penalizes pairwise correlation bound violations in actor loss

equations.name: Bang-bang policy density
equations.role: Factorized Bernoulli; q^i output by actor network via sigmoid

equations.name: Bang-bang entropy regularization
equations.role: Encourages exploration; gamma annealed from 0.01 to 0

tensors.name: X_n (state)
tensors.role: Asset prices at time step n

tensors.name: sigma (volatility)
tensors.role: Volatility vector output of actor policy

tensors.name: a (action / volatility matrix)
tensors.role: Lower-triangular Cholesky factor a = diag(sigma)*L; used in log-Euler step

tensors.name: xi (Gaussian noise)
tensors.role: i.i.d. N(0,I_d) increments for path simulation

tensors.name: V_phi(X_n)
tensors.role: Critic value estimate at current state

tensors.name: m_theta(X_n)
tensors.role: Actor network output: mean of latent Gaussian

tensors.name: Adv
tensors.role: Advantage: discounted next value minus current value

optimizer_hyperparams.schedule: sigmoid annealing (same schedule as exploration param)
optimizer_hyperparams.note: beta1/beta2 not explicitly stated; using PyTorch Adam defaults

batch_size.note: M=2^15=32768 samples; minibatch 2^10=1024; antithetic doubles effective samples

epochs.reason_for_10: Transfer learning from previous time step; fast convergence observed

time_discretization.note: N specified per experiment; value N=192 ensures monitoring dates on grid for Sharpe option

training_pipeline.advantage_normalization: zero mean, unit variance within each epoch
training_pipeline.transfer_learning: Initialize time step n weights from trained time step n+1 weights

evaluation_protocol.datasets: Synthetic (Monte Carlo simulation under UVM model; no external datasets)
metrics: Actor price (lower bound; 95% MC confidence interval, 2^19 paths)
Critic price (pointwise value function evaluation, no bias guarantee)
Runtime (seconds, full backward loop + final MC)

options_tested.name: Best-of butterfly
options_tested.payoff: max(0, X^max_T - K1) - 2*max(0, X^max_T - (K1+K2)/2) + max(0, X^max_T - K2)

options_tested.name: Call Sharpe
options_tested.payoff: max(0, X_T - K) / sqrt(V_T); K=100, V_T = realized monthly volatility
options_tested.regime: fixed correlation (path-dependent; state augmented with A1, A2)

implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper says Adam but does not specify beta1/beta2

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Layer normalization applied to inputs before passing to hidden layer (not post-activation)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper mentions layer normalization to improve stability; standard pre-norm usage

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Sigmoid annealing schedule: lambda(l) = lambda_min + (lambda_max - lambda_min) * sigmoid((l_mid - l) * slope); l_mid ~ E/2
implementation_assumptions.basis: Described as 'sigmoid-type decay' with two phases; Figure 1 shows shape

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Weight initialization: PyTorch default (Kaiming uniform for linear layers)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Not stated; standard for PyTorch ELU networks

implementation_assumptions.assumption: C-vine parameterization follows Joe (2006) / Joe, Kurowicka, Lewandowski (2009) recursive formula for d>=4
implementation_assumptions.basis: Explicitly cited; d=3 formula given in paper; d>=4 recursive

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Correlation penalty evaluated only at mean action (not averaged over stochastic samples) during actor update
State-sampling distribution uses zero correlation between assets (diagonal covariance) for log-normal sampling
No gradient clipping; no weight decay applied
implementation_assumptions.basis: Not mentioned in paper; omission implies not used
alternatives: gradient norm clipping common in RL

ambiguities.description: Exact sigmoid annealing formula not given; only shape described and Figure 1 shown
ambiguities.primary_assumption: f(l) = sigma((l_mid - l) * k) scaled to [lambda_min, lambda_max]

ambiguities.description: Adam beta1 and beta2 not specified
Entropy coefficient schedule for bang-bang policy: initial value 0.01 specified; decay schedule shares sigmoid shape with temperature
